# CSS Consistency Audit Report

**Date**: 2026-02-06
**Scope**: All 19 themes, desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (375x812) viewports, ~145 pages per theme
**Tool**: screenshots generated by `tools/visual-regression.js`

---

## 1. Margin & Padding Issues in Text Boxes / Forms

### Settings page tab truncation (ALL themes, mobile)
The Settings tab bar clips the last tab ("Notifications" truncated to "No..." or "Not...") on mobile across every theme. The tab container needs `overflow-x: auto` or the tabs need to wrap.

### Voting Data form inputs (ALL themes, mobile)
The date/month inputs on the Voting Data page use raw unstyled `<input>` elements with no padding normalization. They appear as small native browser inputs with inconsistent sizing across themes. These should use the `--e2-input-bg`, `--e2-input-border`, and `--e2-input-text` variables with normalized padding.

### Full Text Search input (ALL themes, mobile)
The search text field and button on the Full Text Search page are unstyled native elements. The input has no padding and the button is a plain browser default. Contrast this with the header search bar which is properly styled.

### Sign Up form fields (ALL themes)
The form inputs are styled inconsistently with the rest of the site. They use native browser chrome with no border-radius or padding normalization. Compare with the editor inputs (drafts, settings) which use the design system variables.

### Notelet Editor textarea (ALL themes)
The textarea content area lacks consistent internal padding compared to the Tiptap editor used on the drafts page.

### SQL Prompt / Node Tracker (ALL themes)
These admin pages render raw text with monospace `<pre>` blocks that have no padding normalization. The `<pre>` blocks extend full-width with no max-width constraint, which on desktop creates extremely wide lines.

---

## 2. Mobile View Issues

### Pamphleteer theme - oversized typography on mobile
The Pamphleteer theme uses `font-size: large` and `font-size: 50px` for headings. On mobile, "Look and Feel" and "Other Users" section headers take up the entire screen width with enormous decorative fonts. The `@media screen and (max-device-width: 480px)` rule in the CSS uses the deprecated `max-device-width` rather than `max-width`, so it may not trigger on modern mobile viewports (375px logical width but higher device pixel ratios).

### Pamphleteer Best Users table - horizontal overflow
The Best Users table on Pamphleteer mobile shows the "Rank" column cut off at the right edge with no scrolling mechanism. The large serif font causes the table to exceed viewport width.

### Chatterlight full page (ALL themes, mobile)
The chatterlight page stacks Notifications, Messages, Chatterbox, and Other Users sections vertically. The "Back to Full Site" button at the bottom appears with a solid blue background but the text inside isn't visible in some themes (deep_ice, mikoyan25) - the button text color doesn't adapt.

### Editor toolbar on mobile (ALL themes)
The Tiptap editor toolbar rows on the e2node mobile view show toolbar buttons in a compact grid. The second and third rows of buttons are properly arranged, but on narrower viewports the toolbar buttons don't wrap - they just stop being visible past the edge.

### Print theme - no header/footer on mobile
The print theme strips all navigation (header, footer, nodelets). On mobile, this removes the bottom navigation bar too, leaving no way to navigate the site.

---

## 3. Color Inconsistency Issues

### mikoyan25 (dark theme) - form inputs have white backgrounds
The sign-up form on mikoyan25 uses white input backgrounds (`#ffffff` default) against a black page background. While the theme sets `--e2-input-bg: #202020`, the sign-up form inputs don't pick up this variable. This creates a jarring white rectangle on a black page. The same issue exists for the Full Text Search input and Voting Data inputs.

### mikoyan25 - checkbox visibility
Checkboxes on the Best Users and Settings pages in mikoyan25 are barely visible - they render with default browser styling (white background with dark border) which appears as tiny bright squares on the dark background.

### Deep Ice - input backgrounds blend with page
Deep Ice sets `--e2-input-bg: #99cfff` which matches `--e2-bg-body: #99cfff`. This makes text input fields invisible against the page background - there's no visual distinction between "input area" and "page background." The dropdown on Settings is slightly distinguished by its native browser chrome but text inputs disappear entirely.

### Deep Ice - writeup header low contrast
The `--e2-bg-writeup-header: #44bbff` with `--e2-text-header: #dddddd` creates a light-blue-on-gray-text combination that has poor WCAG contrast ratio (approximately 1.8:1 vs the 4.5:1 minimum).

### Gunpowder Green - writeup header/footer text on dark green
The theme sets `--e2-writeup-header-bg: #114433` and `--e2-writeup-header-text: #ffffff`. This works for the writeup headers, but the social sharing icons (Facebook, Twitter, Reddit) in the writeup footer use hardcoded dark colors that become invisible against the dark green background.

### e2gle theme - link colors don't match theme intent
e2gle uses `--e2-color-link: #0000cc` (bright blue) and `--e2-color-link-visited: #660099` (purple) which are classic web 1.0 colors. However, the nodelet header background is `#d0d0ff` (lavender) with `#000000` text - the active link blue against lavender nodelet backgrounds has poor visual distinction.

### Understatement variants - identical mobile rendering
The five understatement variants (base, cool, warm, bare, monochrome) produce nearly identical mobile screenshots. The color differences (e.g., cool=purple accents, warm=brown accents) are almost imperceptible on mobile because the mobile nav bar and header use the same variables. The differentiating accent colors only show in the nodelet headers, which are hidden on mobile.

### Pamphleteer - epicenter bar blends with page
Pamphleteer sets `--e2-epicenter-bg: #f4f4f4` and `--e2-bg-body: #ffffff`, with `--e2-epicenter-text: #000000`. The epicenter bar is barely distinguishable from the page background - just a subtle gray-on-white difference.

### bookworm/bookwormier - writeup footer icon contrast
Both bookworm themes use muted green/olive tones. The social sharing icons in writeup footers appear in a dark style that doesn't have enough contrast against the olive/sage content backgrounds.

---

## 4. CSS Architecture Issues (Pattern Normalization Opportunities)

### Inconsistent variable coverage across themes

| Variable | basesheet | mikoyan25 | deep_ice | gunpowder_green | understatement | pamphleteer | e2gle |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `--e2-input-bg` | #fff | #202020 | #99cfff | #ffffff | #ffffff | #ffffff | #ffffff |
| `--e2-input-text` | #495057 | (missing) | #000033 | (missing) | (missing) | (missing) | (missing) |
| `--e2-input-border` | #dee2e6 | (missing) | #38495e | (missing) | (missing) | (missing) | (missing) |
| `--e2-card-bg` | #f8f9fa | (missing) | #99cfff | (missing) | (missing) | (missing) | (missing) |
| `--e2-modal-bg` | #fff | (missing) | #99cfff | (missing) | (missing) | (missing) | (missing) |
| `--e2-error-bg` | #ffebee | (missing) | #ffcccc | (missing) | (missing) | (missing) | (missing) |

Many themes only define a subset of variables, falling back to the basesheet defaults. This works for light themes but breaks for dark themes (mikoyan25) where the fallback white backgrounds create jarring contrast.

### Duplicated structural CSS across themes

Every theme file re-declares the same structural patterns:
- Logo override block (`div#e2logo { width: auto; height: auto; ... }`)
- Mobile logo text override (`@media (max-width: 768px) { .e2-mobile-logo-text { display: inline !important; } ... }`)
- Print styles (`@media print { ... }`)
- Typography declarations (`body, table, td, th { font-family: ...; font-size: ...; }`)
- Link styling (`a:link { color: var(--e2-color-link); ... }`)
- Oddrow styling (`.oddrow { background-color: var(--e2-bg-oddrow); }`)

These should be in the basesheet with theme files only overriding variables and truly theme-specific rules.

### Softlink gradients are per-theme inline rules

Every single theme file contains 64 individual `td#sl{N}` background-color rules for the softlink gradient. This could be a single CSS gradient or a few CSS variables (`--e2-softlink-start`, `--e2-softlink-end`) processed in the basesheet.

### Non-variable-based overrides in theme files

Several themes still use hardcoded colors in structural rules:
- Gunpowder Green: `.wu_footer .widget a { color: #009966; }` - hardcoded
- Gunpowder Green: `.nodeshell a { color: #687788; }` - hardcoded
- mikoyan25: `.oddrow { color: #ffffff; }` - hardcoded alongside variable usage
- Deep Ice: `.hasvoted { color: #ffffff !important; }` - `!important` override
- Pamphleteer: `text-shadow: 0 0 1px #667;` - hardcoded accent

---

## 5. Recommended Fixes (Priority Order)

### P0 - Critical (affects readability)

1. **mikoyan25 form input backgrounds**: Ensure all form inputs respect `--e2-input-bg` and `--e2-input-text`. The sign-up, search, and voting data forms use native inputs that ignore theme variables.
2. **Deep Ice input-background-matches-page**: Add a distinct `--e2-input-border` and ensure inputs have visible borders when `--e2-input-bg` matches `--e2-bg-body`.
3. **Deep Ice writeup header contrast**: Change `--e2-text-header` to `#000033` or darken the writeup header text color.

### P1 - High (layout/usability)

4. **Settings tab bar overflow on mobile**: Add `overflow-x: auto` and `flex-wrap: nowrap` to the settings tab container.
5. **Pamphleteer mobile typography**: Fix the `@media` query from `max-device-width` to `max-width` and add responsive heading sizes.
6. **Print theme mobile nav**: Preserve the mobile bottom nav even in print theme, or add an alternative navigation.

### P2 - Medium (visual polish)

7. **Normalize form inputs site-wide**: Create a `.e2-input` class that applies `--e2-input-bg`, `--e2-input-text`, `--e2-input-border`, padding, and border-radius consistently. Apply to all `<input>`, `<textarea>`, and `<select>` elements in the basesheet.
8. **Consolidate softlink gradients**: Replace 64 per-cell rules with CSS `linear-gradient` using `--e2-softlink-start` and `--e2-softlink-end` variables.
9. **Pamphleteer epicenter bar**: Increase contrast between epicenter and page background.
10. **Understatement variant differentiation on mobile**: Ensure mobile nav/header uses accent colors so the variants are visually distinct.

### P3 - Architecture (reduce churn)

11. **Extract shared structural rules to basesheet**: Move logo overrides, mobile logo text, print styles, link styling, oddrow styling, and typography base into basesheet. Theme files should only contain `:root` variable overrides and truly unique structural rules.
12. **Audit variable coverage**: Ensure all 19 themes define the full set of variables from basesheet's `:root`, especially `--e2-input-*`, `--e2-card-*`, `--e2-modal-*`, and `--e2-error-*`. Create a linting check that flags missing variables.
13. **Eliminate hardcoded colors in theme structural rules**: Replace all remaining hardcoded hex values in theme-specific rules with CSS variables.
14. **Create reusable CSS component classes**: Define `.e2-card`, `.e2-form-group`, `.e2-table`, `.e2-alert`, `.e2-button` patterns in basesheet that automatically inherit theme variables. This would prevent each React component from inventing its own one-off styling.
